name: Al-Furqaan Foundation
mission: Al-Furqaan Foundation was established in 2003 with mission to deliver the message of the Qur'an to every individual in America in order to fulfill 5 rights the Quran has upon us as Muslims which are, Believe It, Recite It, Understand It, Act Upon It & Convey It. Al-Furqaan Foundation projects primarily serves USA but recently are also expanding into Canada with future plans to take The Quran distribution project to other English Speaking Countries like U.K, Australia & India InshaAllah. Al-Furqaan Foundation now is been recognized as an international da'wah organization. Al-Furqaan Foundation also works closely with many national interfaith organizations for the betterment of society. While having continuous focus on self-development and outreach, Al-Furqaan Foundation has paved its place as a leading grassroots organization in the American Muslim community.
theoryOfChange: Al-Furqaan Foundation operates with a clear mission to deliver the message of The Quran to every individual in America, aiming to fulfill what they identify as the '5 rights of The Quran' upon Muslims: to Believe It, Recite It, Understand It, Act Upon It, and Convey It. This mission serves as their core strategic framework, guiding their various initiatives. Their work is structured across three primary verticals: Dawah Projects, Educational Projects, and Service Projects, each designed to make the Quran's message accessible. Examples of these projects include The Furqaan Project (Quran distribution), The Clear Islam (street dawah), Islam in Prison, Dawah in Spanish, Dawah in Braille, The New Muslim Foundation, and Furqaan Academy (educational institutions). The organization's vision for the future includes expanding dawah outreach globally and strengthening educational institutions to nurture confident, faithful Muslim leaders, and ensuring new Muslims find a supportive environment.
scoreSummary: AL-FURQAAN FOUNDATION shows good impact and good alignment, with zakat compliance (significant risk deductions applied).
zakatClaimEvidence: Al-Furqaan Foundation explicitly states, "Donate your zakat today." Their donation pages also include an option to select "Zakat/Sadaqa" when choosing the number of Quran boxes to sponsor and as a general donation designation. Masjid Furqaan, a division of Al-Furqaan Foundation, also has a dedicated "Zakat Distribution" section on its website. (Source: https://furqaan.org/giving/)

judge_issues.message: The charity's programs focus on religious education and outreach, which aligns with the 'Fi Sabilillah' (In Allah's Cause) category for religious/educational causes. However, the claimed 'Riqab' category (slaves/human trafficking victims) is not directly supported by the program descriptions.
The charity's programs align well with the 'Fi Sabilillah' category for religious and educational causes. Explicitly stating this alignment could strengthen the classification.

dimension_explanations.alignment: This charity is a fit for donors interested in religious education and media advocacy [3]. It is zakat-eligible and follows specific policies for distributing funds to those in need [2]. Its mission focuses on long-term cultural change rather than immediate crisis relief [1].

While the group holds 25.1 months of working capital, there is limited external evidence to measure how effectively these programs change public perception [1].

rich_narrative.headline: Al-Furqaan Foundation is a religious non-profit dedicated to educating the American public about the core principles of Islam through large-scale distribution of literature and community outreach.

ideal_donor_profile.best_for_summary: Best for donors who value traditional dawah and literature distribution as a means to counter Islamophobia in the United States.
donor_motivations: Want to support the distribution of the Quran and Islamic literature
Seek to fulfill zakat through the fisabilillah category
giving_considerations: The organization has very high cash reserves relative to its annual budget
Governance is centralized with minimal board diversity
Impact is measured by volume of materials rather than behavioral change
ideal_donor_profile.not_ideal_for: Donors seeking high-growth organizations or those who require a large, independent board of directors for governance comfort.

impact_evidence.evidence_grade_explanation: Grade C reflects verified output metrics like <cite id="7">855,697 books distributed</cite>, but lacks independent impact evaluations.
impact_evidence.theory_of_change_summary: The foundation believes that providing authentic Islamic literature to the public and inmates will reduce misconceptions and foster religious understanding.
impact_evidence.why_evidence_matters: Tracking distribution ensures that donor-funded materials are reaching the intended recipients.

long_term_outlook.room_for_funding_explanation: While the foundation has high reserves, its <cite id="6">declining revenue trend</cite> suggests a need for new donor support to maintain scale.

peer_comparison.differentiator: Distinguished by <cite id="5">extremely low fundraising costs</cite> and a <cite id="4">89.3% program ratio</cite>.

strengths.detail: The foundation directs <cite id="4">89.3% of its total expenses</cite> toward its core programs, exceeding common sector benchmarks for efficiency.
The organization maintains <cite id="9">25.1 months of working capital</cite>, providing a significant financial safety net for long-term operations.
With a fundraising efficiency of <cite id="5">$0.01 per $1 raised</cite>, the charity minimizes overhead related to donor acquisition.

strengths_deep_dive: Al-Furqaan Foundation demonstrates a lean operational model that prioritizes direct mission delivery. Its <cite id="4">89.3% program ratio</cite> is supported by a very low <cite id="10">CEO compensation of $32,495</cite>, which represents only about 1% of total revenue. This fiscal discipline allows the foundation to maintain <cite id="11">total assets of over $9.3 million</cite> while continuing to distribute nearly <cite id="7">one million books</cite> to the public and correctional facilities through its 'Islam in Prison' initiative.
rich_narrative.summary: founded in 2003, Al-Furqaan Foundation is a religious non-profit dedicated to <cite id="1">educating the American public about the core principles of Islam</cite>. The organization addresses negative media narratives through large-scale literature distribution and community outreach. Its operations are centered in the United States, where it manages diverse initiatives including the <cite id="2">Furqaan Project, Furqaan Dawah Center, and The Clear Islam</cite>. Financially, the foundation reported <cite id="3">$3,249,437 in total revenue</cite> for the 2020 tax year. A defining characteristic of its financial model is a <cite id="4">program expense ratio of 89.3%</cite>, which indicates that the vast majority of donor funds directly support its educational mission. The foundation also maintains <cite id="5">exceptional fundraising efficiency</cite>, spending only $0.01 to raise every $1. Despite these efficiencies, the organization saw a <cite id="6">revenue decline of approximately 4%</cite> between 2019 and 2020. Its impact is primarily measured through the distribution of materials, with audited records showing <cite id="7">855,697 books distributed</cite> in a single reporting period. The foundation is <cite id="8">zakat-eligible</cite>, specifically serving the category of fisabilillah (in the path of Allah).
